seo.title: Clinical Evidence for Able Assess | Able Care
seo.description: Peer-reviewed research linking grip strength and functional health to falls, frailty, mortality and clinical outcomes. The evidence base behind Able Assess.

blocks.headline: The science behind Able Assess.
blocks.subtitle: Peer-reviewed research linking grip strength and functional health to falls, frailty, mortality and clinical outcomes. Every claim on this site is grounded in the evidence below.

pulledStat.text: Grip strength predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure.

studies.title: Prognostic value of grip strength: findings from the Prospective Urban Rural Epidemiology (PURE) study
Association between muscular strength and mortality in men: prospective cohort study
Grip strength as a predictor of all-cause mortality in older adults: a systematic review and meta-analysis

pulledStat.text: Multifactorial falls risk screening outperforms single-test approaches in predicting falls in older adults.

studies.title: Effectiveness of multifactorial interventions in preventing falls among older adults in the community
Gait speed as the sixth vital sign: a systematic review
studies.source: Journal of the American Geriatrics Society, 2019

studies.title: NICE 2025 guideline: Falls in older people: assessing risk and prevention

pulledStat.text: Low grip strength is associated with increased risk of cardiovascular disease, respiratory disease, renal disease and cancer mortality.

studies.title: Grip strength and cardiovascular disease: a systematic review
Sarcopenia and grip strength in oncology patients: a systematic review
Grip strength as a surrogate marker for GLP-1 related muscle loss in clinical trials

blocks.heading: Four validated metrics in one workflow

metrics.name: Grip strength
metrics.description: Upper-body strength proxy. Predicts all-cause mortality, frailty and hospitalisation risk.

metrics.name: Sit-to-stand
metrics.description: Lower-limb power and balance. A key indicator of fall likelihood and functional decline.

metrics.name: Gait speed
metrics.description: The 'sixth vital sign' for older adults. Linked to rehospitalisation, disability and mortality.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.description: Falls risk assessment in under a minute. Validated across home, community and clinical settings.

blocks.heading: Talk to the clinical team about the evidence.
blocks.body: Our clinical team can walk you through the research and how it applies to your setting.
